refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide detailed worksite area analysis including terrain mapping, obstacle detection, and work-zone representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed into specific sub-functions to address distinct aspects of worksite mapping, enabling focused design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify each sub-FR by demonstrating accurate terrain maps, reliable obstacle detection, and correct work-zone representation through simulation and field tests.
sub_frs: Provide terrain mapping of the worksite area
Detect and represent obstacles within the worksite
Represent designated work-zones within the worksite

candidate_dps: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle data acquisition
Implement a geospatial data processing algorithm for terrain modeling
Use a GIS-based work-zone representation system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle data acquisition

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically addresses the FR with minimal coupling to other DPs; terrain and obstacle data acquisition is a foundational step that can be modularized.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability and integration capability of LIDAR and camera sensors
Risk of sensor data inaccuracies affecting mapping quality
High safety criticality requires robust sensor fusion and validation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding redundancy or alternative sensing methods to mitigate sensor failure risks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical sensor suite and onboard processing for terrain and obstacle data acquisition

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Send processed terrain and obstacle data to NGHE Solution for further analysis and work-zone representation

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Receive configuration updates and send sensor data streams

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as sensors and initial processing are physically on the vehicle; low diffusion risk.
Receives data but does not contain sensor hardware; moderate diffusion due to data processing.
Minimal containment; mainly a consumer of processed data.
No direct containment; may receive alerts but not involved in mapping.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Sensor data acquisition and initial processing are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and ensure real-time data availability.
containment_risks: Potential hardware failure impacting data quality
Limited processing power on Vehicle may constrain algorithm complexity
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate offloading some processing to NGHE Solution if Vehicle processing capacity is insufficient.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some data diffusion to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules, but contained interfaces minimize risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module provides strong containment for sensor acquisition and initial processing.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by testing sensor data acquisition on Vehicle and validating data transmission via Telemetry interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FR to Vehicle IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider redundancy in sensor suite to mitigate safety risks.
Assess processing load on Vehicle and potential need for distributed processing.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments meet the requirements with acceptable risk and clear architectural boundaries. No immediate need to revise or zigzag down.
next_level_sub_frs: Provide terrain mapping of the worksite area
Detect and represent obstacles within the worksite
Represent designated work-zones within the worksite

revise.why: Current FR, DP, and IM assignments are consistent and feasible.

assumptions: Vehicle has sufficient processing capability for sensor fusion.
Telemetry and Network Link interfaces are reliable and secure.
open_risks: Sensor data accuracy under varying environmental conditions.
Potential hardware failures impacting mapping quality.
evidence_needed: Field test data validating sensor fusion accuracy.
